methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Neuromeka is a public robotics and manufacturing company listed on KOSDAQ (ticker 277810.KQ) that has raised $36.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a fragmented peer universe comprising one public comparable and eight private robotics firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics—a total of nine sector peers.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. Despite identifying 11 same-sector robotics and manufacturing peers, none satisfy the minimum threshold of three comparable companies when filtered by stage, adjacent-stage proximity, or valuation tier. The narrow peer set lacks sufficient homogeneity to support a reliable median multiple, and an all-sector median approach would introduce material bias given the heterogeneity of business models and maturity stages across robotics subsectors.

The limited comparable data constrains triangulation. With only one public peer and insufficient private peer clustering, traditional valuation approaches cannot be reliably executed. Any valuation conclusion would require supplementary methods—such as DCF analysis grounded in Neuromeka's specific unit economics and growth profile, or a bottom-up assessment of addressable market and competitive positioning—to establish a defensible range. The key uncertainty is the absence of sufficient narrow-set comparables to benchmark against peer revenue multiples or growth metrics.
